content.text:
Now I have a clear view of the molecule with atom C:10 highlighted. From this image, I can definitively identify the neighbor atoms of C:10 (carbon atom with index 10):

- C:1 (carbon atom with index 1) - directly bonded
- C:5 (carbon atom with index 5) - directly bonded  
- C:9 (carbon atom with index 9) - directly bonded

I can see that C:10 is part of a ring structure and has exactly three covalent bonds to these carbon atoms. There are no hydrogen atoms directly bonded to C:10 in this structure.

The geomconf_index for this molecule is 2154, as indicated by the filename "geomconf_2154.xyz".

Therefore, the neighbor atoms of atom index 10 (C) are:
- Atom index 1, element C
- Atom index 5, element C  
- Atom index 9, element C

Geomconf_index: 2154

question: Please find the neighbour atoms of the atom with atomic index 10, whose element should be C. Here, neighbour atoms are defined as atoms that are covalently connected to this atom. Report all neighbour atoms by listing their atomic index (as shown in the image) and their element. Also return the geomconf_index for this molecule.
